___comment: Hand-curated Great Lakes vessel database. MMSIs verified from live AIS data.

366921230.name: Paul R. Tregurtha
366921230.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
366921230.note: At 1,013 ft the longest vessel ever to sail the Great Lakes — the 'Queen of the Lakes'. Carries iron ore and coal.

366921370.name: James R. Barker
366921370.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
366921370.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader. One of the original 'thousand-footers' built in the 1970s boom. Carries iron ore and coal.

366921380.name: Mesabi Miner
366921380.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
366921380.note: 1,000-foot bulk carrier named for the iron-rich Mesabi Range of Minnesota. Perpetual fixture on Lake Superior routes.

367541380.name: Edwin H. Gott
367541380.note: 1,004-foot self-unloader. Regularly hauls taconite pellets between Lake Superior iron ports and lower lakes steel mills.

367035710.name: Roger Blough
367035710.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367035710.note: 858-foot bulk carrier named for the chairman of U.S. Steel during the industry's postwar boom. A recognizable silhouette on the lakes.

366983830.name: American Integrity
366983830.builder: Fincantieri Bay Shipbuilding, Sturgeon Bay WI
366983830.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader — the first new US-flag laker built in over 35 years when launched. Carries limestone and aggregates.

367541250.name: John G. Munson
367541250.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367541250.note: 768-foot limestone hauler. One of the oldest active vessels on the lakes, still carrying aggregate after 70+ years of service.

316001816.name: Algosoo
316001816.note: 740-foot Equinox-class self-unloader. Part of Algoma's major fleet renewal, built in China for fuel efficiency.

316001815.name: Algoma Harvester
316001815.note: One of the newest Canadian lakers. Grain carrier serving Prairie export terminals via the St. Lawrence Seaway.

316001781.name: Algoma Guardian
316001781.note: 740-foot self-unloader operating on all five Great Lakes. Part of Algoma's modern fleet expansion.

367541110.name: Herbert C. Jackson
367541110.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367541110.note: 690-foot self-unloader. Has moved over 100 million tons of cargo. One of the lakes' most hard-working veterans.

367035690.name: Philip R. Clarke
367035690.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367035690.note: 767-foot bulk carrier. A familiar sight on the St. Clair River for over 70 years hauling iron ore and coal.

338234680.name: American Mariner
338234680.note: 638-foot self-unloader. Regularly hauls limestone and coal between Lake Huron and Lake Erie ports.

338234670.name: American Valor
338234670.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
338234670.note: Self-unloader on coal and limestone routes for over 50 years. A durable veteran of the American Steamship fleet.

367541200.name: Kaye E. Barker
367541200.note: 730-foot self-unloader, formerly the Edward L. Ryerson. Renamed 2013. A Bethlehem-built classic in its eighth decade.

367541090.name: Hon. James L. Oberstar
367541090.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367541090.note: Renamed in 2015 for the late Minnesota congressman who championed Great Lakes transportation for 36 years in Congress.

316011905.name: Algoma Equinox
316011905.note: Name ship of the Equinox class. First of Algoma's new-generation self-unloaders — 30% more fuel-efficient than the vessels it replaced.

316011906.name: Algoma Innovator
316011906.note: 740-foot Equinox-class self-unloader. Carries aggregates and grain across the Great Lakes–St. Lawrence system.

316011904.name: Algoma Mariner
316011904.note: 740-foot Equinox-class self-unloader. Regular transit through the St. Clair River between Lake Huron and Lake Erie.

316011903.name: Algoma Navigator
316011903.note: 740-foot Equinox-class self-unloader. Sister ship of the Algoma Equinox, commissioned the same year.

316001783.name: Algoma Spirit
316001783.note: Self-unloader and one of Algoma's newer vessels. Operates primarily on limestone and aggregate routes.

316001817.name: John D. Leitch
316001817.note: 730-foot self-unloader. Built in Canada in 1967 — one of the oldest active vessels in the Algoma fleet.

316011910.name: Manitoba
316011910.note: New-generation Algoma bulk carrier named for the Prairie province. Grain and aggregate carrier on the Lakes–Seaway system.

316001800.name: CSL Tadoussac
316001800.note: 730-foot self-unloader. Named for the town at the mouth of the Saguenay River. One of CSL's longest-serving stalwarts.

316001801.name: CSL Niagara
316001801.note: 730-foot self-unloader named for the famous falls on the Canada–US border. Carries aggregates and grain between ports.

316011907.name: Whitefish Bay
316011907.note: 740-foot Trillium-class self-unloader named for the scenic bay at Lake Superior's eastern end. CSL's modern workhorse fleet.

316011908.name: Baie St. Paul
316011908.note: 740-foot Trillium-class self-unloader. Named for the charming town on the St. Lawrence. First of the CSL Trillium series.

316011909.name: Thunder Bay
316011909.note: 740-foot Trillium-class self-unloader named for Lake Superior's largest port. Sister ship of the Baie St. Paul.

316003094.name: Rt. Hon. Paul E. Martin
316003094.note: Named for Canada's 21st Prime Minister. Trillium-class self-unloader — PM Martin's family has deep roots in Canada Steamship Lines.

366921210.name: Walter J. McCarthy Jr.
366921210.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
366921210.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader. Named for the Interlake president who led the company through the self-unloader era of the 1970s.

366921220.name: Stewart J. Cort
366921220.note: The first 1,000-foot vessel ever built for the Great Lakes. Pioneered the modern thousand-footer design in 1972.

366921190.name: Lee A. Tregurtha
366921190.builder: Great Lakes Engineering Works, River Rouge MI (rebuilt 1976)
366921190.note: Built in 1942 as the SS Ernest T. Weir, lengthened and rebuilt to 826 ft in 1976. One of the oldest active Great Lakes vessels.

338234640.name: Indiana Harbor
338234640.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
338234640.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader. Among the most active vessels on the Great Lakes, making dozens of limestone runs each season.

338234690.name: American Spirit
338234690.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader. Regular on limestone routes from Lake Huron quarries to lower-lake industrial ports.

338234700.name: American Century
338234700.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
338234700.note: 1,000-foot self-unloader. One of the last classic thousand-footers built, delivering coal and limestone for over 40 years.

338234650.name: H. Lee White
338234650.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
338234650.note: 704-foot self-unloader. Named for the Port of Oswego's long-time director. Carries aggregates and coal across the lakes.

367035610.name: Edgar B. Speer
367035610.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367035610.note: 1,004-foot self-unloader named for the chairman of U.S. Steel. One of the last vessels built by American Ship Building Co.

367035640.name: George A. Stinson
367035640.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367035640.note: 1,004-foot self-unloader named for the chairman of National Steel. Carries taconite pellets on Lake Superior runs.

367541350.name: Cason J. Callaway
367541350.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367541350.note: 767-foot bulk carrier. One of the fleet's longest-serving vessels, still hauling cargo after more than 70 years of service.

367035680.name: William Clay Ford
367035680.builder: Great Lakes Engineering Works, River Rouge MI
367035680.note: 647-foot bulk freighter. Named for the grandson of Henry Ford. One of the older active vessels still transiting the Soo Locks.

367541360.name: Charles M. Beeghly
367541360.builder: American Ship Building Co., Lorain OH
367541360.note: 678-foot self-unloader named for a chairman of Youngstown Sheet & Tube. Carries limestone from Lake Huron quarries.

316003123.name: MNRF Patrol Vessel
316003123.operator: Ontario Ministry of Natural Resources & Forestry
316003123.note: Ontario government research and patrol vessel. Conducts fisheries surveys, environmental monitoring, and search & rescue on Lakes Huron and Erie.

316023959.name: Point Valiant
316023959.note: Canadian harbour tug regularly working the Sarnia–Port Huron waterway and Lake St. Clair. Part of McKeil Marine's tug fleet.
